title: LMCache/LMCache
friendlyTitle: LMCache: faster reuse for LLM inference
summary: LMCache is an open-source KV cache management layer for scalable LLM inference, designed to make cache state persistent, reusable, observable, and transferable across serving engines and storage systems. It targets long-context, multi-turn, agentic, and RAG workloads where repeated prefill work can be expensive.
friendlySummary: LMCache helps LLM serving systems reuse KV cache data across requests, sessions, and engines. That can reduce waiting time and improve throughput for long-context workloads.
whyItMatters: AI infrastructure teams can use LMCache to reduce time-to-first-token, improve throughput, persist KV caches beyond one engine process, and monitor cache behavior in production serving stacks.
friendlyWhyItMatters: It is useful for teams running their own AI infrastructure, especially for agents, RAG systems, and long conversations where repeated computation gets expensive.
